@graph.label: Cor.3.12 (IUTchIII) is a Mochizuki-formulated corollary asserting a multiradial bound on log-volume valid up to indeterminacies Ind1, Ind2, Ind3
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary of the corollary's existence and shape; not an assertion of its mathematical correctness.

@graph.label: IUT proves the abc conjecture (Mochizuki, original 2012)
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level claim attribution; no prose redistribution.

@graph.label: Cor.3.12 reduces to a trivial inequality under harmless simplification (Scholze-Stix 2018)
@graph.specific_objection: After identifying canonical copies of the theta-link, the multiradial algorithm reduces to a tautology incapable of yielding a height inequality (Scholze-Stix 2018, p.10: 'leads to an empty inequality'; p.9: 'this data is canonically the same on both sides').
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with verbatim short quotations.

@graph.label: Distinct copies of Hodge Theaters do not exist in the form required (SS sub-claim 1)
@graph.specific_objection: Scholze-Stix 2018, p.6 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): 'up to equivalence of categories, choosing a Hodge theater is equivalent to choosing a once-punctured elliptic curve abstractly isomorphic to X'; the equivalence of categories implies the distinctness of copies needed for the inter-universal argument is not present at the formal level.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level paraphrase + verbatim short quotation of Scholze-Stix 2018, p.6 (equivalence-of-categories argument).

@graph.label: Anabelian geometry plays no essential role at the disputed step (SS sub-claim 2)
@graph.specific_objection: Scholze-Stix 2018, Remark 9, p.5: 'we could not find the point in the proof where it [anabelian geometry] is essential'.

@graph.label: Fundamental groups and geometry are equivalent and commute, making the IUT operations trivial (SS sub-claim 3)
@graph.specific_objection: Scholze-Stix 2018, p.9 footnote 12 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): 'the critical [IUTT-3, Theorem 3.11] does not become false, but trivial' — under the canonical identifications taken consistently, the multiradial algorithm reduces to a tautology because the fundamental-group / geometry equivalence makes the commutation trivial.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level paraphrase + verbatim short quotation of Scholze-Stix 2018, footnote 12 p.9.

@graph.label: The simplification proposed by Scholze-Stix is not harmless; it destroys the inter-universal un-entangling structure (Mochizuki 2018)
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary.

@graph.label: IUT four papers accepted and published by PRIMS, 2021-03
@graph.fair_use_note: Publication is a factual event; does not constitute external mathematical verification.

@graph.label: A substantial portion of the Western mathematical commentariat publicly remains skeptical of the IUT proof
@graph.fair_use_note: Informal community commentary; not a peer-reviewed consensus statement. Word 'majority' avoided as no formal poll exists.

@graph.label: Arithmetic Teichmuller Spaces yield a corrected framework where abc follows (Joshi v1 2025-04, v2 2026-05)
@graph.specific_objection: Joshi (arXiv:2505.10568v2 abstract): the alleged gap identified by Scholze-Stix in IUTchIII Cor.3.12 can be repaired by reformulating Mochizuki's setup via Arithmetic Teichmüller Spaces (ATS); under ATS, the abc conjecture follows. This is neither a defense of the original IUT proof nor an endorsement of the SS critique as terminal; it is a reformulation.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary of the abstract; full preprint not redistributed. Informal third-party objections (Scholze MathOverflow, Sawin locality critique) have been reported in community channels but stable URLs were not verified at 2026-05-06; pending evidence-add via GitHub Issues with the `evidence-only` label.

@graph.label: LANA project (2026-03-31) is formalizing anabelian geometry and IUT in Lean 4
@graph.status: mid-report-scheduled-2026-07-17; team has articulated a specific contentious point but not yet resolved it (per Mochizuki 2026-04 PDF)
@graph.fair_use_note: Project announcement and progress report; no mathematical verdict yet.

@graph.label: Not Even Wrong (Woit) ongoing skeptical commentary on Mochizuki reports (2025-2026)
@graph.fair_use_note: Blog-level commentary; not peer-reviewed.

@graph.label: Mochizuki 2025-10 IUT progress report continues defense of original framework
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary based on PDF metadata only; full content not redistributed.

@graph.label: Yamashita 2024 survey provides an independent exposition of the IUT proof framework
@graph.fair_use_note: Yamashita is a Mochizuki-aligned proponent; this exposition is a re-presentation, not an independent verification.

@graph.label: Mochizuki's 'Essential Logical Structure of IUT' provides a structured AND/OR logical map of the proof (referenced as background)
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level reference; full PDF not redistributed.

@graph.label: nLab provides a community-maintained semi-formal summary of the IUT theory and dispute
@graph.fair_use_note: External wiki reference, not redistributed.

@graph.label: Anabelian geometry (mono-anabelian framework) is essential at Cor.3.12 (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchI §I3 pp.21-22: the absolute anabelian / mono-anabelian framework allows IUT to look inside one Hodge theater from another; without it, the log-link dismantles ring structure irreversibly.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level claim attribution; no prose redistribution.

@graph.label: General Frobenioid theory (Frd I/II) is essential to the IUT proof, not merely decorative (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchI Thm A p.13: a Hodge theater is 'in essence, a system of Frobenioids'; IUTchI p.13 (before Thm A): the etale-like vs Frobenius-like dichotomy 'will play a key role in the theory developed in the present series of papers'.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with verbatim short quotations from IUTchI.

@graph.label: Global realified Frobenioids from F-circ-times-mu-prime strips are always canonically trivial via gamma_can (Scholze-Stix 2018, p.10)
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 p.10 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): 'always canonically trivial using the various gamma_can' — the canonical trivialization of the global realified Frobenioid, combined with canonical identification of theta-link copies, renders the log-volume inequality empty (no nontrivial constraint survives).
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level claim attribution with verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.10.

@graph.label: Global realified Frobenioid is essential at Cor.3.12 via FrdII Thm 5.5-iii category-theoretic reconstructibility (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: FrdII Theorem 5.5-iii p.58 + IUTchI Remark 3.5.1-ii pp.86-87: category-theoretic reconstructibility of C^R under equivalences; the poly-Frobenioid indeterminacy tracking (Ind1, Ind2, Ind3) is non-trivial and cannot be replaced by working directly with ordered R-vector spaces.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level claim attribution; no prose redistribution.

@graph.label: The global realified Frobenioid actually used at Cor.3.12 is very elementary and equivalent to ordered 1-dim R-vector spaces (Scholze-Stix 2018)
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 p.7 §2.1.4 verbatim: 'the notion of a global realified Frobenioid is very elementary'; 'the category of global realified Frobenioids is equivalent to the category of ordered 1-dimensional R-vector spaces'. SS p.10: global realified Frobenioids from F-circ-times-mu-prime strips are 'always canonically trivial using the various gamma_can'.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with verbatim short quotations from SS 2018 pp.7, 10.

@graph.label: Tempered anabelian rigidity (EtTh Thm 1.6) is invoked by Mochizuki at the abstract pilot stage; SS use it as input without independent evaluation (Scholze-Stix)
@graph.specific_support: SS 2018 fn.5 + p.8 §2.1.8 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): tempered coverings appear as the apparatus on which the theta-pilot construction is based. SS use 'tempered coverings' without independent verification of EtTh Thm 1.6, treating tempered-rigidity as part of the Mochizuki framework they are analysing rather than a step they reject. Note: the SS 'ingenious algorithm' compliment (p.8 §2.1.8) targets the q^{j²}_v recovery algorithm of EtTh Thm 1.10 (handled by claim:absolute_anabelian_theta_acknowledged), not EtTh Thm 1.6 specifically.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level scope statement; correction of the prior version of this claim that misattributed SS p.8 'ingenious' to EtTh Thm 1.6.

@graph.label: Mochizuki's algorithm to recover q_v^{j^2} from tempered coverings (EtTh Thm 1.10) is ingenious and not disputed (Scholze-Stix 2018)
@graph.specific_support: SS 2018 p.8 §2.1.8 verbatim: 'Mochizuki devises an ingenious algorithm to recover this data very directly from the data of pi_1(X) acting on a certain monoid of divisors on tempered coverings of X.' fn.11 notes l-multiplicity issue but does not contest the algorithm.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.8; statement-level attribution.

@graph.label: The F-circ-times-mu-Theta prime strip construction (Frobenioid-theoretic theta values) is accepted by Scholze-Stix at the construction level
@graph.specific_support: SS 2018 p.8 §2.1.8: 'Up to some 2l-th roots of unity, these arise naturally as the values of a Theta-function at certain 2l-torsion points'; SS constructs F-circ-times-mu-Theta strip without objection. SS critique begins downstream at p.9-10 §2.2 (R-copies identification and j^2-scaling monodromy).
@graph.specific_objection: The essential role of EtTh Thm 5.10 at IUTchIII Cor.3.12 is disputed: SS p.10 argues abstract and concrete Theta-pilot objects are canonically identified via gamma_can.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.8; statement-level attribution.

@graph.label: F-circ-times-mu-prime strips on both sides of the Theta-link are canonically the same; only the generator name differs (Scholze-Stix 2018, p.9)
@graph.specific_objection: Scholze-Stix 2018, p.9 §2.1.9 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): 'The F-circ-times-mu-prime strips are given by data of the form Gv acting on o-times-mu_kbar_v times N on both sides (at finite places), and this data is canonically the same on both sides. It is simply the name of the generator of the monoid N that appears that is called Theta respectively q.' If this canonical identification is admitted, the full poly-isomorphism collapses to an obvious isomorphism. SS p.9 footnote 12 (verbatim): 'the critical [IUTT-3, Theorem 3.11] does not become false, but trivial.' The 'empty inequality' phrasing belongs to SS §2.2 p.10 main text, not to footnote 12.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from Scholze-Stix 2018 p.9 for academic critique documentation.

@graph.label: The full poly-isomorphism between theta-strip and mod-strip is not reducible to a canonical isomorphism; the two sides inhabit distinct ring-theoretic structures (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchI Rmk 3.7.1 p.89: the Theta-link identifies F-circ-theta and F-circ-mod only via a full poly-isomorphism of abstract topological monoids, not as rings. The SS canonical identification implicitly uses the ring structure - precisely what the inter-universal framework holds separate across distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures. Accepting SS canonical identification is alleged to destroy the un-entangling property essential to the multiradial algorithm.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level paraphrase; no substantial prose reproduction.

@graph.label: F-star-l symmetry (NF-side multiplicative) is essential in IUT; Scholze-Stix is silent on this structure (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: Mochizuki IUTchI §4 (Prop 4.8-4.9, Rem 4.7.2, 4.9.2) + §5 (Def 5.5, Cor 5.6) + §6 (Rem 6.12.3, 6.12.6): F-star-l = F-times-l / {pm1} acts simply transitively on the label set J of the Theta-NF-Hodge theater. This multiplicative arithmetic symmetry is structurally distinct from F-pm-ell symmetry of the Theta-pm-ell side. F-star-l is essential for explicit F_mod number field moduli structure (Prop 4.7-iii), the 'global combinatorial resolution of additive + multiplicative dimensions of Z' (Rem 6.12.6-v), and the unique gluing of the Def 6.13 triple. SS 2018 (10 pp PDF-verified): F_l-star / multiplicative symmetry / number field (in symmetry context) all 0 occurrences. SS treats Theta-pm-ellNF-HT as a unit equivalent to X (§2.1.2 p.6); internal NF-bridge / F-star-l structure is neither analyzed, verified, nor denied.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level claim attribution; SS-side silence documented via full-text PDF verification.

@graph.label: Alien copies framework is the structural counter to SS canonical identification: ring structure is obliterated across the inter-universal wall (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchI Rem 3.9.4 p.94 (verbatim): the distinct n+1 q_v belongs to a distinct scheme theory from the base nq_v; the indeterminacy in the isomorphism between associated isomorphs of D-vdash 'obliterates the ring structure - the arithmetic holomorphic structure - associated to nD_v for distinct n.' Alien Copies §3.3-vii: Theta-link is 'fundamentally incompatible with the ring structures in its domain and codomain'. This obliteration is the structural reason why the SS canonical identification (p.9: 'canonically the same on both sides') is illegitimate at the inter-universal step: the two F-circ-times-mu-prime strips reside in distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotations from IUTchI p.94 and Alien Copies p.73 for academic documentation.

@graph.label: SS 2018 analyzes F-vdash-times-mu strip construction (Def 4.9) at the output layer of Cor 4.10; Mochizuki Kyoto agreement covers only strip description, not canonical identification conclusion
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 §2.1.5 pp.7-8 (PDF-verified): F-circ-times-mu strip data described as G_v acting on o-times-mu times N. SS p.4 'Mochizuki agreed that some of these simplifications are OK, for example regarding the critical notion of F-circ-times-mu-prime strips below.' Mochizuki (RIMS 2018 response) contests that this agreement covers only the description of strip data, NOT the legitimacy of the subsequent canonical identification step (SS §2.2 p.10). Hodge-Arakelov evaluation (Cor 4.10-ii) declared out of scope: SS §2.1.2 fn.5 p.5 'plays no role for us'. Theta-gau-link: 0 occurrences in SS 2018.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotations from SS 2018 p.4 and p.5 fn.5.

@graph.label: The Theta-gau-link is essential to the IUTchIII multiradial algorithm (Thm 3.11); outside SS 2018 analytical scope (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchII Cor 4.10-iii p.160: Theta-gau-link = composite of Theta-times-mu-link with inverse of Hodge-Arakelov evaluation. IUTchII Rmk 4.10.2-i p.162: 'it is precisely by thinking of [a further enhanced version of] the Theta-times-mu-gau-link as an object that one may establish the crucial multiradiality properties discussed in [IUTchIII], Theorem 3.11.' Value-group transformation q_v to {q^{j²}_v}_{j=1,...,l-star} is the operative Teichmuller deformation content. SS 2018 (10 pp PDF-verified): 0 occurrences of Theta-gau / Gaussian link; SS reduces to F-circ-times-mu output layer without engaging the internal composite structure.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with direct quotation of IUTchII Rmk 4.10.2-i p.162.

@graph.label: Among prime-strip variants, Scholze-Stix 2018 analyzes only F-circ-times-mu in depth; all other variants (D, F-top, LGP, procession) are outside SS analytical scope
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 PDF full-text (pymupdf-verified 2026-05-06): 'prime strip' occurs 15 times concentrated in §2.1.5-2.1.9 on F-circ-times-mu; F-top (vdash) 0 occurrences; LGP 0 occurrences; procession 1 occurrence (§2.2 summary-only). SS p.4 Excuse (1): 'Mochizuki agreed that some of these simplifications are OK, for example regarding the critical notion of F-circ-times-mu-prime strips below' - the exact scope of agreement is disputed (no public minutes). SS Footnote 9 p.8: 'times-mu-Kummer structure plays no role for us'.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotations from SS 2018 p.4 and p.8 footnote 9.

@graph.label: Procession is essential for Ind1 indeterminacy reduction in the multiradial algorithm (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchI Prop 6.9-i: procession reduces label-indeterminacy from (l-pm)^(l-pm) to (l-pm)! possibilities. IUTchIII Theorem A (Cor 3.12): the multiradial algorithm is a functor in Prc(D-top); Ind1 = permutation automorphisms of the procession diagram. The procession-normalized mono-analytic log-volume (averaging over j in F-star-l) makes the log-volume estimate finite. SS 2018 mentions procession once (§2.2, p.9) without engaging the Ind1 reduction argument; SS does NOT explicitly characterize procession as a 'notational convenience' (PDF-verified 2026-05-06).
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary; SS-side scope documented without straw-man framing.

@graph.label: Inserting the j-squared scalars into the diagram of Cor.3.12 produces a monodromy j-squared, i.e. an inconsistency (Scholze-Stix 2018 p.10)
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 p.10 §2.2 (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): when the canonical identifications are taken consistently, the j-squared scalars must be omitted from the equation, otherwise the diagram acquires a 'monodromy j-squared, i.e. being inconsistent'. Either choice (omit -> empty inequality; keep -> inconsistent diagram) prevents the proof from delivering arithmetic content.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.10 for academic critique documentation.

@graph.label: Mochizuki's blurring response requires blurring of order O(l-squared), rendering any inequality so obtained useless (Scholze-Stix 2018 p.10)
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 p.10 §2.2 final paragraph (PDF-verified 2026-05-06): on day 5 of the Kyoto discussions Mochizuki proposed that indeterminacies (Ind1, Ind2, Ind3) blur the diagram enough to absorb the j-squared problem; SS reply that the required blurring is of order O(l-squared), which would render the resulting inequality useless for arithmetic applications.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.10 for academic critique documentation.

@graph.label: The log endofunctor is naturally equivalent to the identity (Scholze-Stix 2018)
@graph.specific_objection: SS p.6 §2.1.3: 'It follows that the endofunctor log is naturally equivalent to the identity, with the natural equivalence to the identity given by log : log(K) ~= K.' Footnote 8 p.7: 'there is a completely natural isomorphism Pi_1 = pi_1(X) = Pi_2 and K_1 ~= K_2 as K_1 = k-bar = K_2 [...]. Choosing these obvious isomorphisms did not result in any problem.' Construction-level (bijection + endofunctor) accepted; SS claims the full poly-isomorphism indeterminacy adds no non-trivial transport.
@graph.fair_use_note: Short verbatim quotations from SS §2.1.3 p.6 and fn.8 p.7; PDF-verified.

@graph.label: log-Kummer correspondence (IUTchIII Thm 3.11-ii) is essential at Cor.3.12; SS dispute targets Cor.3.12 conclusion not Thm 3.11-ii construction (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchIII Thm 3.11-ii establishes log-Kummer correspondence as a system of Kummer isomorphisms across the vertical log-theta-lattice: (a) tensor packets are upper-semi-compatible (Ind3) with log-link composites, (b) splitting monoids are precisely compatible (no indeterminacy), (c) global Frobenioid MOD-version is precisely compatible (mod-version is not). This system is the mechanism by which Cor.3.12's log-volume estimate becomes a non-trivial bound. SS PDF (10pp pymupdf-verified): 0 occurrences of 'log-Kummer correspondence'; SS critique targets the Cor.3.12 conclusion (downstream of Thm 3.11-ii) rather than Thm 3.11-ii construction itself. Out of SS analytical scope.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary of IUTchIII Thm 3.11-ii.

@graph.label: log-theta-lattice is non-commutative; horizontal Theta-mu-link and vertical log-link generate the inter-universal framework (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchIII Def 1.4 + Rem 1.4.1-i: 'the various squares are far from being commutative'. Mechanism: vertical log-link is constructed from local-ring-dependent power series; horizontal Theta-times-mu-link is essentially incompatible with the same local ring structure (IUTchII Rem 1.11.2). Result: 'horizontal arrow execution necessarily mandates vertical arrow execution' (Rem 1.4.1-ii). Witt-vector / Frobenius intertwining analogy in Fig 1.3 (Rem 1.4.1-iii). SS PDF: 1 occurrence of 'log-theta-lattice' (paper title in references only); structural non-commutativity outside SS analytical scope.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with verbatim short quotation from IUTchIII Rem 1.4.1.

@graph.label: log-shell containment O-sharp-K subset I_v is essential for the Cor.3.12 log-volume estimate (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: IUTchIII Prop 1.2-v establishes O-sharp-K subset I_v (containment b_non/c_non); Rem 1.2.2-iii establishes upper semi-compatibility of coric log-shells with composites of log-link iterates. Together these constitute the foundation for the log-volume upper bound estimate in Cor.3.12 (IUTchIII §3). SS PDF (10pp pymupdf-verified) contains 1 occurrence of 'log-shell' at §2.2 p.9 only; SS performs no structural analysis of Prop 1.2 / Rem 1.2.2 - these propositions are outside SS scope.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary of IUTchIII Prop 1.2-v and Rem 1.2.2-iii.

@graph.label: The Kyoto agreement (Mochizuki accepting some SS simplifications) covers the description of F-circ-times-mu-prime strip data, not the canonical-identification step that SS subsequently use (Scholze-Stix 2018 p.4)
@graph.specific_objection: SS 2018 p.4 (verbatim, PDF-verified 2026-05-06): 'During our discussion in Kyoto, Mochizuki agreed that some of these simplifications are OK, for example regarding the critical notion of F-circ-times-mu-prime strips below.' Mochizuki's 2018 Report on Discussions disputes that this agreement extends to the canonical identification step at SS §2.2; the precise scope of the Kyoto agreement is itself contested.
@graph.fair_use_note: Verbatim short quotation from SS 2018 p.4.

@graph.label: F-pm-ell symmetry is essential in IUT; SS is silent on this structure (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: Mochizuki IUTchI §6 Remark 6.12.3 and 6.12.6: the F-pm-ell symmetry (additive, label set bijects with F_l, simply transitive) is essential for the Hodge-Arakelov evaluation of the etale theta function in IUTchII, operating on a single geometric basepoint at each v in V. Structurally distinct from F-star-l symmetry (multiplicative, NF side). Scholze-Stix 2018 mentions 'Theta-pm-ellNF-Hodge theater' as a type label (p.6 §2.1.2-2.1.3) but does NOT independently analyse the internal F-pm-ell sub-structure. SS p.6 simplifies the entire Theta-pm-ellNF-HT to 'choosing a once-punctured elliptic curve abstractly isomorphic to X' via equivalence of categories, thereby bypassing the additive symmetry / cusp-label / sigma-symmetry analysis rather than rejecting it. Verified by pymupdf full-text scan of SS 2018 (10 pp): 0 occurrences of 'F_l-pm', 'additive symmetry', 'sigma-symmetry', 'cusp label', 'LabCusp-pm'.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary with verbatim short quotation of SS 2018 §2.1.2 p.6.

@graph.label: All three mono-theta rigidities (cyclotomic, discrete, constant multiple) are simultaneously essential to the IUT theta-link and Cor.3.12 multiradial algorithm (Mochizuki)
@graph.specific_support: EtTh Cor 2.19 (pp.58-59): all three rigidities established for mono-theta environments; bi-theta environments fail discrete rigidity (Cor 2.16 / Rem 2.16.1). IUTchII §2: theta-link requires cyclotomic comparison across Hodge theaters. IUTchIII Cor.3.12: multiradial algorithm invokes functorial choice-free algorithms of Cor 2.19 as foundational input. SS 2018 全文 (pymupdf-verified) に 'mono-theta' / 'Cor 2.19' の出現なし; SS 批判は downstream の R-copies identification に集中し、3 rigidities apparatus を invalid とする主張なし.
@graph.fair_use_note: Statement-level summary; SS-side silence documented in specific_support.
